atomicity.message: Staged payload is exactly the lint parallel-execution bead: bounded deterministic command scheduling, CLI --jobs wiring, and direct tests; unrelated VORCH/Beads files remain unstaged.

conditions.message: The staged lint runner changes are formatted, scoped to executable lint command behavior, and mechanically satisfy the lint-rule surface.
evidence: ScarletMarsh verified git diff --cached --name-status listed only cmd/burpvalve/main.go, internal/backpressure/lint.go, and internal/backpressure/lint_test.go.
ScarletMarsh verified the packet hash command returned 10fed5fee997086a3c9cf908eafe468bcbd443d284591280a902d5dddd1a2966 and git diff --cached --check produced no output.

ScarletMarsh inspected staged lint.go: RunLint passes LintOptions.Jobs into runLintCommands, stores results by command index, caps default parallelism at command count/4, preserves jobs=1 serial mode, and runs Serial commands after the parallel phase.

conditions.message: The staged implementation centralizes scheduling in one lint runner helper and preserves existing command execution/result formatting helpers rather than duplicating lint semantics.
evidence: BrightOwl inspected git show :internal/backpressure/lint.go and found RunLint delegates executable commands to runLintCommands(ctx, root, executable, opts.Jobs), then evaluates failures from the returned manifest-order result slice.
BrightOwl verified runLintCommands is the only new scheduling helper; runLintCommand still owns command execution, buffering, timeout, env, run_directory, and status mapping.
BrightOwl staged-snapshot go test ./... passed with only cmd/burpvalve/main.go, internal/backpressure/lint.go, and internal/backpressure/lint_test.go staged.

conditions.message: Parallel execution does not weaken lint evidence: results stay tied to each manifest command, multiple failures are all reported, optional failures remain advisory, and output is buffered per command rather than live-interleaved.
evidence: BrightOwl verified br show project-readiness-kit-lint-parallel-execution-a9w --json requires bounded deterministic parallel execution, jobs=1 serial behavior, manifest-order output, independent timeouts, serial:true phase-two scheduling, and no sequential-help claim.

BrightOwl inspected staged lint.go: results is pre-sized to len(commands), workers write results[item.index], and RunLint builds required/advisory failure accounting from each LintCommandResult rather than short-circuiting.
BrightOwl inspected staged lint_test.go: TestRunLintParallelReportsMultipleFailures and TestRunLintParallelTimeoutDoesNotCancelOtherCommands cover multiple failures and sibling timeout isolation.

conditions.message: The new concurrent lint behavior has direct behavioral tests for the key scheduler and CLI job paths.
evidence: ScarletMarsh inspected staged lint_test.go and found tests for manifest-order preservation under parallelism, multiple required failures, timeout isolation, serial commands after the parallel batch, jobs=1 serial behavior, and default bounded parallelism.
ScarletMarsh inspected staged cmd/burpvalve/main.go and verified CLI/robot paths pass --jobs into RunLint and defaults changed to 4.
ScarletMarsh clean temp worktree tests passed: go test ./internal/backpressure -run TestRunLint.*... and go test ./cmd/burpvalve -run Test.*LintJSONCompat|Test.*JobsFlag|Test.*LintInit -count=1.

conditions.message: The staged payload satisfies the high-level lint-parallel contract: jobs is wired from CLI/robot to RunLint, default/high jobs are bounded, jobs=1 is serial, serial:true runs after the parallel batch, deterministic order is tested, and full staged-snapshot tests pass.
evidence: BrightOwl verified git diff --cached --name-status exactly M cmd/burpvalve/main.go, M internal/backpressure/lint.go, M internal/backpressure/lint_test.go.

BrightOwl reproduced the packet hash command and observed 10fed5fee997086a3c9cf908eafe468bcbd443d284591280a902d5dddd1a2966; Burpvalve canonical staged payload hash observed was sha256:0d436ac8a043c29ceb6756d6007010dba1aa7d85f94c199e7373660ae377111f.
BrightOwl staged-snapshot targeted lint/cmd tests, race-targeted internal/backpressure tests, full go test ./..., and git diff --cached --check all passed.

conditions.message: Evidence is explicit and bound to the packet staged diff hash.
evidence: Packet 2710 supplied feature id, packet kind, exact diff-hash command, expected diff hash, payload paths, assigned conditions, and implementer-side focused/full test claims.

ScarletMarsh independently ran git diff --cached --name-status, git diff --cached --stat, git diff --cached --check, the packet hash command, staged source inspection via git diff --cached and git show, focused tests, full internal/backpressure test, and full go test ./... in a clean temp worktree.
ScarletMarsh recorded that live package tests were avoided in favor of a clean staged-slice worktree because unrelated unstaged files were reported in the packet.

conditions.message: The staged slice is limited to lint parallel execution, CLI jobs/help wiring, and tests; unrelated dirty VORCH/Beads/backpressure files are not staged or part of the verified temp tree.
evidence: BrightOwl verified git diff --cached --stat for the three paths reports 3 files changed, 242 insertions, 11 deletions.
BrightOwl verified git diff --cached --name-status lists only cmd/burpvalve/main.go, internal/backpressure/lint.go, and internal/backpressure/lint_test.go.

BrightOwl verified git status --short showed .beads/issues.jsonl, cmd/burpvalve/beads_close_test.go, internal/backpressure/artifacts.go, verifier_responses.go, and verifier_responses_test.go unstaged/untracked and not included in staged-snapshot verification.

conditions.message: No destructive operation surface is added beyond running already-declared lint commands, and the risky scheduling change is bounded with serial support.
evidence: ScarletMarsh inspected the staged payload and found no deletes, resets, force pushes, migrations, external sends, production mutations, or new command definitions; it changes scheduling of existing lint_commands only.
ScarletMarsh verified concurrency is bounded by jobs and capped to command count / 4 by default when unspecified or too high; CLI rejects jobs <= 0 before running lint.
ScarletMarsh verified Serial commands are excluded from the parallel channel and run after the parallel batch, preserving an escape hatch for commands that must not overlap.

conditions.message: Result ordering, failure semantics, timeout isolation, and serial command constraints are preserved under concurrency.
evidence: ScarletMarsh inspected staged runLintCommands: each command result is written to results[item.index], so result order remains manifest order even when execution order differs.

ScarletMarsh verified serial commands are skipped in the parallel feed and filled into original indexes after the parallel batch, preserving manifest-order output while allowing declared ordering constraints.
ScarletMarsh verified failure aggregation iterates result.Commands and uses each commandResult.Required/ID/Error, preserving required vs optional semantics after concurrent execution.

conditions.message: The concurrency change does not widen command authority: it executes the same manifest-declared shell commands through the existing runLintCommand path, keeps run_directory normalization and per-command timeout/process-group kill behavior, and only adds bounded scheduling over already-validated commands.
evidence: BrightOwl inspected staged lint.go and verified executableLintCommands validation and run_directory normalization remain before scheduling; runLintCommands receives already validated lintconfig.Command values.

BrightOwl verified runLintCommand still uses exec.CommandContext(ctx, sh, -c, command.Command), sets BACKPRESSURE_LINT_PATHS, sets process group, and kills only the command process group on cancellation.

BrightOwl verified each command gets context.WithTimeout(parent, timeout); a child timeout does not cancel the parent context shared by sibling commands, and race-targeted staged-snapshot tests passed.

conditions.message: The staged payload changes CLI help/robot text, backend lint command scheduling, and Go tests. It does not touch browser UI, screenshots, responsive layout, canvas, image assets, or terminal rendering components beyond plain help strings for jobs.
evidence: ScarletMarsh verified staged payload paths are only cmd/burpvalve/main.go, internal/backpressure/lint.go, and internal/backpressure/lint_test.go.
ScarletMarsh inspected the staged diff and found no visual assets, CSS/layout code, Bubble Tea/lipgloss rendering changes, screenshots, or browser surfaces.

conditions.message: The payload is explicitly performance-motivated and bounded; tests cover both default parallel speed and serial fallback.
evidence: ScarletMarsh inspected runLintCommands: it allocates a result slice sized to command count, caps default worker count to at most 4 and no more than command count, uses a WaitGroup and channel for non-serial commands, then runs serial commands after the parallel phase.

ScarletMarsh verified TestRunLintDefaultParallelismIsBounded would fail if two 0.35s commands ran sequentially beyond its 900ms threshold, and TestRunLintJobsOneRunsSerially verifies explicit serial execution when jobs=1.
ScarletMarsh clean temp worktree focused lint parallel tests and full go test ./... passed.

conditions.message: Agent-facing behavior remains deterministic and explicit: robot/help text no longer claims sequential execution, --jobs is documented as bounded concurrency, and JSON/human command results remain in manifest order regardless of completion order.
evidence: BrightOwl inspected git diff --cached -- cmd/burpvalve/main.go: robot input docs now describe jobs as bounded concurrency defaulting to 4/capped by command count, lint init robot notes no longer say execution remains sequential, and runLint passes Jobs into backpressure.RunLint.

BrightOwl inspected staged lint_test.go: TestRunLintParallelResultsRemainInManifestOrder asserts slow/fast completion still returns slow,fast in manifest order; TestRunLintSerialCommandsRunAfterParallelBatch asserts serial:true phase-two scheduling while preserving result order.

BrightOwl CLI validation smoke observed go run ./cmd/burpvalve lint --root <tmp> --json --jobs 0 returned rc=1 with stderr lint --jobs must be positive; staged-snapshot targeted tests and full go test ./... passed.
